days.title: Tokyo — Shibuya · Harajuku · Asakusa

ideas.title: Shibuya Sky rooftop viewpoint
ideas.notes: Open-air observation deck on Shibuya Scramble Square. 360° Tokyo views. Book midnight 30 days in advance.

ideas.title: Shibuya 12F hidden viewpoint (free)
ideas.notes: Free secret viewing area behind the teppanyaki restaurant on the 12th floor of Shibuya Scramble Square. No one talks about it.

ideas.title: Harajuku thrift: Laforet / Loveless / Atmos / Beauty & Youth
ideas.notes: Best Harajuku stores for youth fashion, streetwear and mid-range basics. Dense — allow 2 hours minimum.

ideas.title: 2nd Street / Mode Off / Treasure Factory (thrift chains)
ideas.notes: Large-format vintage and second-hand chains. Great for clothing, records, accessories. Very local experience.

ideas.title: Don Quijote Shibuya
ideas.notes: Largest Don Quijote in Japan. Open 24/7. Less crowded after 10pm. Classic Japan experience — everything under one roof.

ideas.title: Omoide Yokocho (Memory Lane) — yakitori alley
ideas.notes: 2 min walk from Keio Plaza. Tiny smoky yakitori bars. Cash only. Classic Tokyo first evening.

ideas.title: Tir na nÓg underground bar, Ginza
ideas.notes: Underground cocktail bar. Cover charge likely ¥500–1,000pp. Check sign at entrance.

ideas.title: [JACK] Parco Shibuya
ideas.notes: 10-floor mall near Shibuya crossing — young, hip, cultural hub. Floor 6 is the draw: Nintendo Tokyo (Japan's first), Pokémon Center Shibuya, Capcom Store, SEGA Store, Godzilla Store. Serious queues on floor 6 — go early or expect 30–60 min waits. Floors 1–5 have excellent fashion (Acne Studios, APC, Brain Dead, AMBUSH, OUR LEGACY). Chaos Kitchen basement = winding alleyway food court. Rooftop terrace on floor 10. 5 min walk from Shibuya Station Hachiko exit.

ideas.title: [JACK] Super Bookoff / Bookoff Super Bazaar
ideas.notes: Japan's biggest second-hand chain with 850+ stores. The Super Bazaar format is enormous — books, manga, CDs, retro video games, vintage clothing, instruments, branded goods, designer bags (authenticated). All items are cleaned and graded. Tax-free with passport on purchases over ¥5,000. Best Tokyo branches: Shibuya (fashion-forward), Shimokitazawa (curated vintage), Akihabara (6 floors, retro games and manga). Good for Japanese-label vintage and cheap CDs.

ideas.title: [JACK] 2nd Street
ideas.notes: Japan-wide chain of second-hand stores. Stock varies dramatically by neighbourhood. Harajuku/Shimokitazawa branches are best for vintage fashion — good for Japanese-label pieces, Americana, Levi's, workwear. Downstairs section at Harajuku branch is particularly strong. Also in Osaka (Amerikamura area). Prices mid-range — not thrift-cheap but fair for what you find.

ideas.title: [JACK] Amam Dacotan — artisan bakery & café
ideas.notes: Omotesando / Kita-Aoyama. One of Tokyo's most famous and talked-about bakeries — extraordinary bread, sandwiches and sweets. Two locations close together: main bakery at 3-7-6 Kita-Aoyama; café & bake at 3-6-23 Kita-Aoyama (1F). Both a few minutes from Omotesando Station Exit B2. Always a queue — arrive early. Perfect combination with Harajuku/Omotesando day.

ideas.title: [JACK] Pain des Philosophes — croissants
ideas.notes: Kagurazaka, Shinjuku City. Selected Tabelog Top 100 bakery 2022. Consistently called the best croissant in Tokyo — proper French technique, buttery and flaky with Japanese touches. Signature furikake-filled baguette is unusual and excellent. Pain au chocolat and Asama loaf also outstanding. Opens 9am, always has a queue, often sells out by noon. Tiny shop — go early, eat in the nearby park. 107-2 Yaraicho, Shinjuku City.
ideas.mapQuery: Pain des Philosophes Kagurazaka Shinjuku Tokyo

ideas.title: [JACK] Laitier — soft serve & desserts
ideas.notes: Sendagaya, Shibuya-ku. French-inspired dessert café — premium soft serve ice cream and sweets. 1-22-7 Sendagaya 1F. Near Sendagaya Station — easy detour between Harajuku and Shibuya.

ideas.title: [JACK] Nephew — café-bar, carrot cake
ideas.notes: Tomigaya, Shibuya-ku — the cool, local Yoyogi-Hachiman / Yoyogi-Koen neighbourhood. Tiny house-style bar and sweets café. 1-7-2 Tomigaya. Jack specifically recommends for the carrot cake. Very local, off the tourist map — good escape from main strips.

ideas.title: [JACK] Poem Mano a Mano Coffee — fruit sandwiches
ideas.notes: Koenji, Suginami — a few minutes from Koenji Station on the Chuo Line. Classic old-school kissaten (Japanese coffee shop) style café. Famous for exceptional filter coffee, French toast and especially fruit sandwiches — seasonal fruit (Shine Muscat, mango, others) in soft shokupan with generous fresh cream. Regularly highlighted as a must-order. Koenji itself is the best thrift/vintage neighbourhood in Tokyo — pair this with a wander through the vintage shops and backstreets. Very local, retro, indie atmosphere.
ideas.mapQuery: Poem Mano a Mano Coffee Koenji Tokyo

days.title: Tokyo — Sumo · Tsukiji · Ueno

ideas.title: Sumo Museum (free)
ideas.notes: Free entry. In the Ryogoku Kokugikan sumo stadium. Good intro to sumo history and culture. Near the Oshiage stable area.

ideas.title: Chankonabe (sumo stew)
ideas.notes: Traditional sumo wrestler diet. Restaurants near Ryogoku station. Hearty, warming, very local. About ¥1,500–2,500pp.

ideas.title: TeamLab Planets (Toyosu)
ideas.notes: Immersive digital art. Barefoot through water. Otherworldly. ⚠ Book 2 months in advance — 9am slots are hardest to get.

ideas.title: Tokyo Skytree observation deck
ideas.notes: Tallest structure in Japan. Observation deck + Solamachi shopping at the base. Book midnight, 30 days in advance.

ideas.title: Shimokitazawa neighbourhood
ideas.notes: Very un-touristy. Strong coffee culture, unique thrift shops. Short train from Shinjuku. Very different vibe to central Tokyo.

ideas.title: Gotokuji Temple (lucky cats)
ideas.notes: Hundreds of ceramic maneki-neko (lucky cat) figures. Very photogenic. Near Shimokitazawa.

ideas.title: Standing sushi bar
ideas.notes: Must-do Tokyo experience. Look for ones near Tsukiji or around major train stations. Fast, cheap, excellent quality.

ideas.title: Ichiran Ramen
ideas.notes: Solo-booth tonkotsu ramen. 24 hours. You order on a sheet, food arrives through a hatch. No social pressure. Classic.

ideas.title: [JACK] Age.3 Ginza — fried sweet sandwiches
ideas.notes: Ginza 1-24-11 Sugiura Building 1F. Takeout-only specialty shop for deep-fried sandwiches — bread fried to order, then immediately filled. 30+ flavours, sweet and savoury. Must-order: Crème Brûlée (torched custard, very good), Chocolate Cream Strawberry, Ganache Matcha. Savoury options: Beef Yakiniku (excellent), Egg Salad. Around ¥850 each. Queue 30–60 min, especially weekends — go early morning or off-peak. Park across the street for eating. Takeout only, no seating.

ideas.title: [JACK] American Diner Andra — bechamel burger
ideas.notes: Higashi-Ueno, Taito-ku — close to Ueno and Asakusa. Signature dish: smash burger cut in half and lowered into a hot iron skillet of sizzling béchamel-style cheese sauce. Theatrical, flavourful, messy. American diner décor (sports jerseys, 80s videos, red/white/blue). 5 Chome-13-7 Higashiueno. Lunch 11am–2pm, Dinner 5pm–9pm. Closed Sundays. Very small (4 tables + 4 counter seats) — arrive before opening or expect a wait. Sign the name book outside first.

ideas.title: [JACK] Ginza Hachigo — Michelin ramen
ideas.notes: ⚠️ BOOK THIS NOW — see checklist. Only 6 seats. Michelin-recognised French-trained chef makes extraordinary clear-broth ramen: duck, chicken, prosciutto, shellfish — no soy tare. Light, velvety, complex. Signature: Ajitama Chuka Soba with soft egg + tender chashu. Special version adds foie gras and truffle dumpling (+¥300). Side chashu rice worth it. Reservations via TableCheck open Saturdays 9am Japan time for following week. Walk-up: queue from 9am, ~30 bowls per day, sells out fast. Ginza 3-14-2.

ideas.title: [JACK] Wagyu Mafia — katsu sando
ideas.notes: Nakameguro, 2 min from Nakameguro Station. Standing counter, 6 stools. Wagyu/Kobe beef chateaubriand deep-fried and served in milk bread — the katsu sando elevated to absurd levels. Multiple cuts available: Sirloin (¥5,000), Chateaubriand (¥18,000+), limited Kobe Champion (¥110,000 — 20 per year, sells in 10 mins). Jack probably isn't after the ¥110k one. The sirloin is the move. Open 11am–9pm. Closed Wednesdays. Nakameguro Atlas Tower 1F, 1-26-1 Kamimeguro.
ideas.mapQuery: Wagyu Mafia Cutlet Sandwich Nakameguro Tokyo

ideas.title: [JACK] The Front Room — milk bread pudding
ideas.notes: Marunouchi Building 1F, 2-4-1 Marunouchi, Chiyoda-ku — directly beside Tokyo Station. All-day café and bakery in an office tower. Jack specifically recommends the milk bread pudding. Great stop if passing through Tokyo Station (e.g. on the way back from a day out).
ideas.mapQuery: The Front Room Marunouchi Tokyo Station

ideas.title: [JACK] Benizuru — Japanese pancakes
ideas.notes: Nishi-Asakusa, Taito-ku. Pancake-focused brunch café. 2-1-11 Nishi-Asakusa. Walkable from Asakusa and Tawaramachi Stations — good add-on to Senso-ji visit. Jack recommends for the pancakes (Japanese-style thick hotcakes).

ideas.title: [JACK] Fukushimaya — oden, daikon radish
ideas.notes: Azabu-Juban, Minato-ku — 2 min from Azabu-Juban Station. Founded 1921. Over 100 years of oden. Ground floor is takeaway fish cakes (satsuma-age); second floor is a cosy izakaya. Signature: miso oden (Maruya Haccho Miso), daikon radish soaked black in broth, beef tendon. Jack mentions fried radish — likely the daikon tempura side dish. Lunch sets very good value. English menu available.

ideas.title: [JACK] Izumo — from Osaka (Okayama day trip)
ideas.notes: Jack mentions Izumo — this is almost certainly Izumo Taisha, the ancient Shinto grand shrine in Shimane Prefecture, one of Japan's most sacred sites. It is a significant detour from your route (4–5 hrs from Osaka by Shinkansen + limited express). Not realistic on the current itinerary. However, if Jack means Okayama Day Trip instead — Himeji is already Day 13. Confirm with Jack what exactly he means.

ideas.notes: 15 min uphill from Bamboo Grove. ¥1,000pp includes matcha tea. Mountain views. Less crowded than main Arashiyama sights.

ideas.notes: Special night illumination during cherry blossom season in April. Book ahead — separate ticket from daytime entry.

items.notes: From hotel: walk to Kawaramachi Station, Hankyu to Kyoto, then JR Sagano Line to JR Saga-Arashiyama (17 min, ¥240pp). Torokko Saga Station is adjacent to JR Saga-Arashiyama Station south exit — 1 min walk.
Go now — this is one of the most crowded spots in Japan during cherry blossom season. 08:00 is genuinely quiet. Nonomiya Shrine is hidden inside the grove — small black torii, very atmospheric. Free.
UNESCO World Heritage garden. Cherry blossoms over the pond. ¥500pp for garden, ¥300 extra for main hall. Allow 45 mins.
1 min from JR Saga-Arashiyama Station south exit. Collect e-ticket at gate via the mobile voucher link — do NOT use the printed PDF.

items.activity: Sagano Romantic Train — Torokko Saga → Kameoka
items.notes: ✅ BOOKED · 4 adults · Car 3 · ¥3,520 paid · E-ticket: linkt.to/oGdvHgf1 — show screen at ticket gate, not the PDF · Arrives Torokko Kameoka ~10:27
items.detail:
Booking ref: ARS_SAGANO_KANKO-20260323-4F63 · Supplier ref: I-260323-1106180 · Traveller: Andy Evans · Route: Torokko Saga (嵯峨駅) → Kameoka (亀岡駅) · Date: Sun 12 Apr 2026 · 4 adults · ¥3,520 paid · Purchased: 23 Mar 2026

E-TICKET: linkt.to/oGdvHgf1 — click this link before boarding and show the screen at the ticket gate. The printed/PDF voucher CANNOT be used for boarding.

Car 3 is an enclosed car with opening windows. The No.5 "Rich" open-air car has no glass — great views but you may get wet in rain, and umbrellas are not allowed onboard.

Journey: 25 minutes through the Hozugawa River gorge. 7.3km of preserved 1989 railway line. Cherry blossoms line the gorge in April.

Boarding: Torokko Saga Station is 1 min from JR Saga-Arashiyama Station south exit. Business hours open ~08:35. Timetable: sagano-kanko.co.jp/en/train-info

Seat changes allowed up to 1 day before departure (max 3 changes).

items.activity: Arrive Torokko Kameoka — shuttle to boat dock
items.notes: Free shuttle bus from Torokko Kameoka Station to Hozugawa boat dock (~15 min). Alternatively a horse-drawn carriage connects the two. Departures timed to the train arrivals.
items.mapQuery: Hozugawa River Boat Ride Kameoka Kyoto

items.notes: ✅ BOOKED via Klook · £99.93 · 4 adults (Andrew, Sarah, Jack, Charlie Evans) · Booking: KLOOK-20260323-DB5V · Participation time: 11:00 · Allow up to 30 min wait at dock before departure · Arrives Arashiyama ~13:15 · Show smartphone QR code ticket at reception desk on arrival
items.mapQuery: Hozugawa River Boat Ride Kameoka Kyoto
items.detail:
Booking ref: KLOOK-20260323-DB5V · Provider: 保津川下り（保津川遊船企業組合）
Travellers: Andrew Evans, Sarah Evans, Jack Evans, Charlie Evans · 4 adults
Date: Sun 12 Apr 2026 · Participation time: 11:00 · £99.93 paid via Klook · Purchased: 23 Mar 2026

TICKET: Show smartphone QR code at the reception desk at the boarding area.
Location: 2 Shimo-Nakajima Hozu-cho Kameoka, 621-0005, Kyoto
Google Maps: https://goo.gl/maps/8pULEHy6xMJswXDj8

DEPARTURE: Regular departures at 9:00 / 10:00 / 11:00 / 12:00 / 13:00 / 14:00 / 15:00
Your slot: 11:00 — allow up to 30 minutes after scheduled time before the boat departs.
If you arrive late for your check-in time, boarding may not be permitted.

WHAT'S INCLUDED: 2-hour boat ride along the Hozugawa River with skilled boatmen.
Life jackets compulsory — provided at the boarding point.
No restrooms on the boat — use facilities before boarding.
Food and beverages are permitted on board.
Photography allowed — inform boatman if you intend to stand up.

WEATHER: Runs in light rain (covered by canopy). May be suspended in severe weather or river flooding.
Check operation status on the day: hozugawakudari.jp

ACCESS FROM TOROKKO KAMEOKA: Free shuttle bus from Torokko Kameoka Station to the boat dock (~15 min). Horse-drawn carriage also available. Departures timed to the train arrivals.
Alternatively: 10 min walk from JR Kameoka Station (North exit).

items.notes: Boat docks just below Togetsukyo Bridge. 5 min walk to Arashiyama Nishiki restaurant on Nakanoshibashi island.

⚠ BOOK AHEAD — Sunday cherry blossom season. Traditional kaiseki ryotei on Nakanoshibashi island beside Togetsukyo Bridge. Sakura-juku-zen seasonal box-set in April. ☎ 075-871-8888 · Hours 11:00–21:00 L.O. 19:00 · Closed Tuesdays · 4 min from Hankyu Arashiyama Station
15 min uphill walk from the bridge. Villa of 1920s samurai film star — stunning gardens over the Kyoto valley. ¥1,000pp includes matcha and tea. Worth it if energy allows after the cruise.
Riverside wander, shops, Tenryu-ji garden exit, Togetsukyo Bridge photos with the mountains.
Hankyu Arashiyama Line from Arashiyama Station → Katsura → Kawaramachi (35 min, ¥270pp). Or JR from Saga-Arashiyama → Kyoto Station (17 min, ¥240pp).
⚠ BOOK AHEAD — Sunday evening cherry blossom season, this will be very busy. Riverside kawayuka terrace dining. Book as soon as possible.

ideas.notes: 9-floor mall built on the former Osaka baseball stadium footprint. Directly connected to Nankai Namba Station. 120+ shops and 40+ restaurants. Famous for the tiered terraced rooftop garden (Parks Garden) with 400+ plant species — free to walk. Fashion floors 1–5, restaurants floors 6–8. Good views of Osaka from upper levels. Opening hours: shops 11am–9pm, restaurants 11am–11pm.

ideas.notes: Twin towers with floating observatory ring. ¥1,500pp. Great at sunset. Only 15 min walk from Candeo hotel.

ideas.title: Hozenji Yokocho alley
ideas.notes: Tiny atmospheric alley near Dotonbori. Moss-covered Fudo statue, candlelit restaurants. Perfect slow pre-dinner wander.

ideas.title: Den Den Town (Osaka Akihabara)
ideas.notes: Osaka's electronics and anime district. Retro game shops, Gunpla (Gundam models), anime figures. Jack and Charlie in their element.

ideas.title: Kushikatsu — DO NOT double dip
ideas.notes: Shinsekai is the home of kushikatsu. Deep-fried breadcrumbed skewers. ⚠ Cardinal sin to double-dip the communal sauce. ~¥200 per skewer.

ideas.title: Kobe beef dinner (splurge night)
ideas.notes: One high-end wagyu meal. Worth it at least once. Look for certified Kobe beef restaurant. ¥8,000–15,000pp. Book ahead.

ideas.title: Melon pan ice cream, Dotonbori
ideas.notes: Freshly baked melon bread with soft-serve inside. Very photogenic. Very Osaka.

ideas.title: [JACK] Acorn Vintage — Osaka
ideas.notes: Described by experts as simply the most curated high-end vintage shop in Japan. Very small and selective — around 200 pieces at a time, only the finest examples: sun-faded M65 jackets in three perfect sizes, big-E Levi's in wearable condition, French moleskin workwear, faded hoodies. Not cheap, but genuinely exceptional. Worth hunting down the address (search Tabelog or Instagram @acorn_osaka before visiting).

ideas.title: [JACK] 2nd Street — Osaka
ideas.notes: Multiple locations in Osaka. Amerikamura and Shinsaibashi area branches are best. Same chain as Tokyo but different stock — worth checking. Good for mid-range vintage fashion and Japanese-label finds.

ideas.title: [JACK] Super Bookoff — Osaka
ideas.notes: Large Bookoff locations in Namba and Shinsaibashi. Good for retro games, manga, vintage clothing. Tax-free over ¥5,000 with passport.

ideas.title: [JACK] Onegi-chan — pickles
ideas.notes: Recommended by Jack for Japanese pickles (tsukemono). Could be a market stall, specialist pickle shop or konbini find. Exact location unclear — ask Jack or search Tabelog closer to the trip.

ideas.title: Himeji leather goods
ideas.notes: Himeji has a centuries-old leather craft tradition. Unique souvenirs along the castle approach street.

ideas.title: Anago (sea eel) lunch in Himeji
ideas.notes: Himeji's local speciality. Restaurants on Otemon-dori approach to the castle.

ideas.title: Spa World (back in Osaka)
ideas.notes: Enormous onsen complex in Shinsekai. Themed floors — Roman baths, Japanese onsen. ¥1,000pp. Ideal after a long Himeji day.
